concepts.name: Machine Learning Operations
concepts.description: An engineering discipline synthesizing monitoring, automation, and governance to manage non-deterministic ML systems in production.

concepts.name: Silent Model Failures
concepts.description: Gradual degradation of predictive accuracy due to real-world changes that go undetected by traditional infrastructure health metrics.

concepts.name: Boundary Erosion
concepts.description: The dissolution of system modularity where changes propagate unpredictably through implicit data dependencies rather than explicit code interfaces.

concepts.name: Correction Cascades
concepts.description: A failure pattern where patching one ML component introduces statistical instabilities requiring endless downstream corrections.

concepts.name: Training-Serving Skew
concepts.description: Accuracy degradation caused by divergent feature computation logic or data distributions between offline training and live production environments.

concepts.name: Feature Store
concepts.description: Centralized infrastructure that computes features once and serves them identically to both batch training and real-time inference pipelines.

concepts.name: Model Registry
concepts.description: A centralized, version-controlled repository managing trained model artifacts, evaluation metrics, and deployment state.

concepts.name: Continuous Training Pipelines
concepts.description: Automated workflows that balance compute costs against accuracy by retraining models based on defined schedules or statistical drift triggers.

concepts.name: Staleness Cost Function
concepts.description: An economic model that quantifies the financial impact of model accuracy decay over time to determine optimal retraining intervals.

concepts.name: Canary Deployment
concepts.description: A risk-mitigation strategy that routes a small percentage of live production traffic to a new model to validate performance stability.

concepts.name: Shadow Deployment
concepts.description: Executing a candidate model on real-world traffic in parallel with production without exposing its predictions to end users.

concepts.name: Model Format Optimization
concepts.description: Converting trained models into specialized, hardware-efficient interchange representations like ONNX or TensorRT to accelerate inference.

concepts.name: Model Quantization
concepts.description: Reducing memory bandwidth requirements and increasing inference throughput by mapping floating-point model weights to lower-precision integers.

concepts.name: Hardware Memory Hierarchy
concepts.description: The multi-tiered memory architecture spanning L2 cache, high-bandwidth memory (HBM), and system RAM that dictates inference latency.

concepts.name: KV-Cache Bottleneck
concepts.description: A memory capacity limitation in LLM serving where storing attention keys and values per sequence restricts maximum batch size.

concepts.name: Concept Drift
concepts.description: A degradation in model performance caused by shifts in the underlying mathematical relationship between inputs and the target variable.

concepts.name: Data Drift
concepts.description: A divergence in the distribution of input features over time while the fundamental relationship to the target remains constant.

concepts.name: Population Stability Index
concepts.description: A statistical distance metric used to quantify distributional shifts between expected training frequencies and actual production serving data.

concepts.name: Metric Cardinality
concepts.description: The combinatorial explosion of unique monitoring labels that dominates the computational and storage costs of system observability.
